description_long: This government building features a long, rectangular structure with a steeply pitched gabled roof and a symmetrical facade. The exterior is constructed from gray stone or brick, with prominent vertical pilasters and decorative stonework outlining the windows and doors. The windows are tall and narrow, arranged in regular intervals along the length of the building, and topped with arched lintels. The main entrance is centrally located on the longer side, marked by a set of double doors and a modest set of steps, flanked by small landscaped areas. The roof is adorned with a pattern of lighter tiles along the edges, adding visual interest to the otherwise utilitarian design. The building sits on a raised foundation with a brown base, suggesting durability and permanence.
description: A long, gray stone government building with a steep gabled roof, arched windows, and a central entrance flanked by pilasters. Decorative stonework and a raised foundation give it a formal, institutional appearance.
description_view_0: The gabled end of the building is visible, showing two large arched windows and detailed stonework above the entrance area.
description_view_1: The long side of the building is shown, featuring the main entrance with double doors, multiple tall arched windows, and landscaped greenery along the base.
description_view_2: The opposite gabled end mirrors the first, with two large arched windows and similar decorative stonework, but no visible entrance.
description_view_3: The other long side of the building is visible, similar to view 1 but without the main entrance, showing a continuous row of arched windows and minimal landscaping.
attribute_public_entrance_design: The main public entrance features a set of double doors framed by decorative pilasters and accessed by a short set of steps. The entrance is understated, with symmetrical stonework and small landscaped areas providing a formal yet accessible approach.
